goal: 20–35 minutes per session, 3 planned sessions/week (2 strength + 1 cardio/movement).
Session timing: ~30 minutes per session, three sessions per week before work.
Weight loss goal: lose ~10 pounds over the next 2–3 months; improve stair endurance and reduced windedness.
constraints: Limit tracking to essential metrics; avoid overwhelm.
Limit tracking to 3 metrics: sessions per week, one performance metric, and one wellbeing metric.
Knee safety: avoid high-impact loading; track knee symptoms; adjust plan if pain or swelling occurs.
Cardio modality preference: prioritize low-impact options (bike/elliptical/rower/swim/walk) and avoid jumping, intense plyometrics, or long-distance running.

Weekend unpredictability: main workouts should be maintained on weekdays (Mon/Wed/Fri); weekends must not be relied upon for core sessions and can be used for optional micro-workouts or family-friendly activity.
decisions: Calorie deficit target: ~500 kcal/day to aim for 0.5–1 lb/week.
Protein intake: ~0.7–1.0 g per lb bodyweight per day, spread across meals.
Workout structure: maintain 3 sessions/week (Mon/Wed/Fri) with Strength A (Mon), cardio/conditioning (Wed), and Strength B (Fri).
Optional shorter sessions: 20 minutes on days with more time.
Cardio plan: low-impact modalities (bike, walking, pool); one cardio day per week initially; stair-specific strength (step-ups).
Tracking: weigh weekly; photos every 2–4 weeks; monitor stairs breathlessness; track consistency (≥3 workouts/week).
Knee safety: warm up adequately; favor knee-friendly exercises; stop if sharp pain or swelling; consult clinician if concerned.
Progression: increase reps/round/tempo for strength; extend cardio duration or intervals gradually; re-test stair performance every 2–3 weeks.
entities: Nutrition plan: modest calorie deficit (~500 kcal/day), high protein with 0.7–1.0 g/lb, focus on lean proteins, veggies, whole grains, and limit liquid calories.
Workout schedule: Monday — Strength A (30 min) with warm-up and 2–3 rounds; Wednesday — Low-impact cardio/conditioning (20–35 min); Friday — Strength B (30 min).
Optional short sessions: 20-minute recovery or mobility sessions on days with time.
Progression: strength progression via +1–3 reps, +1 extra round, or +5 minutes cardio; cardio progression via longer intervals or more total time; stair performance test every 2–3 weeks.
Tracking approach: weekly weigh-ins; periodic photos; note stair breathlessness and overall energy; target consistency of ≥3 workouts/week.
Knee safety reminders: warm up well; avoid high-impact; use knee-friendly substitutions; stop if pain or swelling persists.
Lifestyle focus: ensure adequate sleep 7–8 hours; hydration; manage stress; energy intake aligned with deficit.
Weekend plan: keep main workouts on weekdays; treat weekends as optional with micro-workouts to maintain momentum when time allows.
open: Open: if persistent knee swelling, instability, or sharp pain occurs, consult a physiotherapist or physician for progressions and clearance.
